authority.primary: SQLite session/event journal and authenticated session API

executionPolicy.releaseGatePassCondition: Every required physical-device row has PASS evidence; PENDING, FAIL, or missing evidence blocks Android Web Push release.

gates.reason: No Android Chrome physical-device execution evidence has been recorded.
This documentation-only change does not execute browser automation; automation cannot satisfy Android physical-device delivery/lifecycle scenarios.

scenarios.title: Install PWA in Android Chrome
scenarios.environment: Android physical device, Chrome, HTTPS same-origin deployment
preconditions: Clean or documented prior installation state
steps: Open the HTTPS origin in Android Chrome.
Install the PWA using Chrome's install flow.
Launch the installed app and navigate to notifications settings.
expected: No session content is shown before normal authentication.
Push opt-in is shown only when secure-context, service-worker, API-support, and owner checks pass.
Installation is not treated as notification permission or subscription consent.

scenarios.title: Allow and deny notification permission
scenarios.environment: Android physical device, Chrome, installed PWA
preconditions: Notification permission is undecided or reset
steps: Choose Allow in the notification opt-in flow and observe registration.
Reset permission or use a fresh profile, choose Don't allow, and observe the UI and server behavior.
expected: Allow attempts an owner-scoped subscription registration.
Deny creates or reuses no subscription and does not claim delivery success.
Session state remains available through the authoritative API/SSE path.
evidenceRequired: Owner-scoped registration outcome without subscription material

scenarios.title: Force-stop behavior
scenarios.environment: Android physical device, Chrome or installed PWA
steps: Force-stop Chrome/PWA from Android Settings.
Cause a permitted notification event.
Reopen the app and inspect the authoritative session state.
expected: Delivery timing or absence is not used as correctness evidence.
On reopen, normal authentication and SQLite/API/SSE recovery converge on authoritative state.
Any notification tap re-enters normal authentication/authorization.

scenarios.title: Doze delivery and recovery
scenarios.environment: Android physical device capable of Doze
preconditions: Device has entered documented Doze state
steps: Leave the device in Doze state.
Cause a permitted notification event.
Wake the device and reopen the app.
expected: Delayed or absent push is not marked as a session failure or delivery success.
After resume, authoritative API/SSE state converges without relying on push order or presence.

scenarios.title: Subscription/token replacement
preconditions: A documented Chrome/site-data action can cause re-registration
steps: Cause normal subscription replacement through Chrome/site-data lifecycle.
Re-enable or re-register notifications as required.
Attempt owner-isolation checks using a different authenticated owner.
expected: Replacement is stored only for the authenticated owner.
Previous record is revoked/disabled and is not selected for new sends.
A different owner cannot read, replace, revoke, or use either record.
evidenceRequired: Owner-scoped server outcome without subscription values

scenarios.title: User and browser permission revocation
scenarios.environment: Android physical device, Chrome, installed PWA
steps: Disable notifications through app UI and observe server-side revoke outcome.
Re-register, then revoke Chrome/Android site notification permission.
Cause a permitted notification event and inspect sender outcome without payload capture.
expected: Revoked records are excluded from sends.
Re-enable requires explicit opt-in and fresh registration.
Permanent provider failure (404/410) causes immediate invalidation rather than retry or false success.

scenarios.title: Notification tap deep-link reauthentication
scenarios.environment: Android physical device, Chrome, notification available
preconditions: A permitted notification contains an opaque session reference and same-origin allowlisted relative deep link
steps: Tap while logged in as the subscription owner.
Repeat after logout or session expiry.
Repeat while logged in as a different owner.
Attempt a non-allowlisted or cross-origin deep-link input through the applicable test surface.
expected: Only same-origin allowlisted route opens.
Logout/expiry requires normal login before protected content.
Different owner receives server-side authorization denial and no session content.
Invalid or cross-origin target is rejected/falls back safely without external navigation.
